entry.title: South China Morning Post: 5 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 5 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-05 04:03AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Offers on Hong Kong brands, seafood, luxuries up for grabs in summer as part of tourism board initiative to boost economy
  • New Hong Kong leader John Lee says local national security law will not be rushed so all future risks can be covered
  • 1 in 5 arrests by Hong Kong’s national security police made under colonial-era sedition offence instead of 4 crimes laid out in Beijing-imposed law
  • Chinese University of Hong Kong to set up academic centre in Shanghai district, in first such collaboration with local government
  • ‘A sadness too great for words’: tributes to Ni Kuang, Hong Kong author and screenwriter, pour in from around the world
  • Hong Kong leader John Lee raised HK$11.3 million in cash from 58 pro-Beijing groups for election campaign
  • More than 100 staff owed HK$5 million in unpaid wages, contributions after Hong Kong restaurant operator abruptly shuts down
  • Hong Kong gets a shot in the arm as China’s offshore financial hub with new products and deeper yuan pool in expanded Connect scheme
  • HKJC kicks off ‘Drago Cavallo’ exhibition to spread positivity
  • Coronavirus: families, couples divided by Hong Kong border with mainland China ‘could reunite for Chinese Valentine’s Day if cases are reduced’
  • Cash stolen after lockers in Hong Kong police changing room pried open
  • Man, 28, remanded in custody for alleged murder of yoga instructor at 5-star hotel in Hong Kong
  • Hong Kong man, 66, arrested for alleged cruelty to 6 rabbits
  • Rescuers find bodies of 12 crewmen suspected to belong to vessel that split in 2 near Hong Kong during Typhoon Chaba
  • Canada diplomats denied access to tycoon Xiao Jianhua’s China trial
  • US-China relations: economic chief Liu He, Treasury Secretary Janet Yellen hold ‘pragmatic, frank’ exchange
  • China’s services activity expands at fastest in almost a year in June
  • China tiptoes towards living with Covid-19 under evolving ‘dynamic zero’ policy
  • China’s GDP target ever more distant, with second quarter growth tipped for 2-year low
  • Joe Biden may roll back China tariffs this week, US media report says
  • Foreigners are leaving China in droves. Does the nation want or need them any more?
  • US and Russian ambassadors to China clash over Ukraine war at Beijing forum
  • New Omicron strains better at dodging protection from vaccines and previous infection, study says
  • Footprints in China show dinosaurs came in from the cold to rule the world, say scientists
  • China takes aim at Nasa chief’s moon military takeover claims
  • Chinese-Canadian tycoon Xiao Jianhua, who disappeared from Hong Kong hotel, to stand trial in mainland China
  • Chinese firm prepares to hand over new US$140 million parliament to Zimbabwe
  • Chinese frigate chased Russian warship away from Diaoyu Islands to stake territorial claim, Japan says
  • Singapore’s flying taxi dreams ready for take-off at its ‘other’ airport
  • Asia-Pacific leaders at Nato summit a sign of strategic shift amid Russia, China threats: analysts
  • Singapore President Halimah Yacob tests positive for Covid-19
  • Coronavirus: Thailand discourages discounts, wants high value tourists post-Covid
  • Malaysian ‘superhero’ film Mat Kilau a hit as tale of betrayal hits close to home
  • Singapore urged to halt hanging of Malaysian drug trafficker
  • Will Pakistan’s Taliban ties blunt its crackdown on anti-India jihadists?
  • Thousands evacuate from ‘dangerous’ Sydney floods
  • Japan launches study to try to reduce number of underweight young women
  • Japan protests as first Chinese navy ship in 4 years spotted near Diaoyu Islands
  • Witnesses recount Chicago July 4 mass shooting in painful, chilling detail
  • Rusty Eiffel Tower needs full repairs, gets US$63 million paint job instead
  • Police arrest suspect after gunman kills 6 at US July 4 parade in Chicago
  • Ukraine estimates cost of post-war reconstruction at US$750 billion
  • Ukraine war: Putin orders Russian troops to fight on after key Ukraine city taken
  • US says Al Jazeera journalist Shireen Abu Akleh likely shot by Israel but not intentionally
  • Ukraine war: blue and yellow flag of Ukraine raised again on Snake Island, military says
  • Ukraine war: Iran slashes cost of oil to gain foothold in China and compete with Russia
  • Coronavirus: Mecca in Saudi Arabia welcomes 1 million worshippers for largest haj pilgrimage since pandemic
  • More Americans are becoming anti-vaxxers as conspiracy theories about Covid-19 vaccines spread
  • Hong Kong stocks climb to one-week high as report US is set to remove China tariffs lifts recovery hopes
  • TSMC chemicals supplier warns of additional chip material price hikes, which could squeeze semiconductor giants
  • Tasty discounts: China property firms accept watermelon, garlic, peaches as partial payment for new homes amid housing slump
  • Hotels set for recovery in Asia-Pacific as borders reopen but China’s decision to remain shut clouds overall picture
  • Why US and European central banks could be source of next global financial crisis
  • Sell-off at Chinese AI giant SenseTime shows no signs of letting up as traders rush for exit to pre-empt insider selling
  • Founder of China car giant Geely completes acquisition of phone maker Meizu, touts synergy between consumer electronics, EVs
  • Cryptocurrency: China selfie app owner Meitu warns of heavy losses after a meltdown in bitcoin and ether prices
  • Mainland investment banks shell out big bucks for top talent as they match Wall Street rivals on pay, IPO deals
  • Why inland regions hold key to China’s continued ‘common prosperity’ push
  • China, Hong Kong regulators to launch Swap Connect, give offshore holders of US$553 billion in onshore yuan bonds more tools to hedge risks
  • Hong Kong’s New World Group introduces four and a half day work week for better work-life balance
  • China NFTs: Tencent and Ant Group join industry pledge to ban cryptocurrency and fight speculation
  • Climate Change: China Power International Development acquires US$1.12 billion in clean-energy assets, eyeing transition goals
  • Building intelligent, sustainable enterprises in Hong Kong
  • ‘Cryptoqueen’ Ruja Ignatova added to FBI’s most wanted list, but she was already notorious in China
  • How our growing belief in sentient machines could be a problem – a Google software engineer thinks his company chatbot AI is self-aware
  • When Hong Kong’s first privately built multi-storey car park opened in 1967, with firecrackers and a fashion show to mark the occasion
  • What is one’s ‘first language’ in Hong Kong when Cantonese, English and others can meld together in the mind?
  • Composer Frank Wildhorn feels a ‘romantic connection’ with Korean audiences, and with four of his musicals playing in Seoul, the feeling appears mutual
  • The pros and cons of airbrush make-up – perfect, flawless looks without a brush or sponge as long as you’re willing to pay for it
  • Mahler, Tchaikovsky and Bruckner on Jaap van Zweden’s schedule for Hong Kong Philharmonic’s 2022-23 season
  • Extreme organising: how decluttering my kitchen helped me find peace of mind, and bring order amid the chaos of life
  • Kim Kardashian, Bella Hadid’s Schiaparelli looks are the work of Daniel Roseberry, ‘unknown’ American who revived the legacy fashion brand
  • What travel in Ukraine is like amid the war – my unusual trip to Odesa
  • STYLE Edit: What timepieces do movie stars wear? Zoë Kravitz, Han So-hee and Zhou Dongyu, three female ambassadors from Omega’s newest celebrity campaign, unveil their choice of women’s watches
  • Has Floyd Mayweather blown a US$1.2 billion fortune? Jake Paul claims the boxer is ‘broke’, after he splashed out on multiple Rolls-Royces, Mercedes, Lamborghinis, Porsches, mansions and private jets
  • Inside Mark Wahlberg’s blinging luxury watch collection, from the Patek Philippe Nautilus Tiffany & Co. to the Rolex Daytona Rainbow, also loved by Kevin Hart and John Mayer
  • 10 richest tennis stars of 2022 – net worths, ranked: from Rafael Nadal and Serena Williams’ Nike deals to Novak Djokovic and Andy Murray’s Adidas gigs – but which retiree earned over a billion?
  • Who is Jackie Chan’s estranged daughter, Etta Ng Chok-lam? The love child of the martial arts star and Elaine Ng, she married influencer Andi Autumn and once claimed to be ‘homeless’ in Hong Kong
  • Meet Harmony Tan, the tennis star who beat Serena Williams at Wimbledon 2022: the French 24-year-old has Cambodian and Vietnamese roots, and ranks No 115 in the world
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: Washington Post: 5 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Hundreds rescued as Sydney suffers fourth flood in less than 18 months
  • Ukraine unveils $750 billion recovery plan as Russia sets sights on Donetsk
  • U.S. concludes unintentional Israeli fire likely killed American journalist
  • Ice avalanche kills 6 in Italian Alps, sparks mass rescue effort
  • Twelve bodies recovered from Chinese ship sunk by typhoon
  • 3 dead, 4 seriously hurt in Copenhagen mall shooting

  • Hurricane Bonnie steams westward off Mexico's Pacific coast
  • Sydney floods impact 50,000 around Australia's largest city
  • Mexican bishops to put photos of dead priests in churches
  • Cardinal Hummes, close friend of Pope Francis, dies at 87
  • A migrant survivor of the trailer: 'They couldn't breathe'
  • Chile president receives draft of new proposed constitution
  • Jihadi attacks kill 22 people in Burkina Faso, officials say
  • Nicaragua government takes over five opposition-held towns
  • Dior does folklore in Paris couture, riffing on Ukraine art
  • Canada sets $15B deal over Indigeneous child welfare flaws
  • Highland Park shooting: Police arrest ‘person of interest’ after gunman killed 6 at parade
  • As gunfire explodes in Chicago suburb, ‘nowhere is safe’
  • Garland weighs racial equity as he considers death penalty in Buffalo
  • Clifford Alexander Jr., first Black secretary of Army, dies at 88
  • 50 people arrested in Akron protests after police released shooting video
  • Trauma physicians have become depressingly prepared for mass shootings
  • Florida teen’s leg to be amputated after weekend of shark attacks
  • Akron police release video of officers shooting Black man dozens of times
  • People searching for abortion online must wade through misinformation
  • How long will your kitchen renovation take?
  • Heir ponders tax implications on inheritance if split with sibling
  • Studio condominium in Arlington lists for $258,000
  • Your Old Fridge Is Vladimir Putin’s Friend. Dump It!
  • Chipmakers and Congress Play a $52 Billion Game of Chicken
  • China Has Yet to Learn the Rules of the Pacific Chess Game
  • Indonesia’s Shuttle Diplomacy Can Be More Than Empty Miles
  • Understanding Monkeypox and How Outbreaks Spread
  • Why Saving the Climate Requires a Tough Taxonomy: QuickTake
  • Why Japan Will Struggle to Do Without Russian Energy
  • Why I will never live without a dog again
  • After the abortion ruling, digital privacy is more important than ever
  • Lincoln’s political party is recklessly testing his wisdom
  • NASA is right to examine UFOs
  • Think democracy isn’t endangered? Just look what happened in Hong Kong.
  • This July 4, let’s declare our independence from the Founding Fathers
  • 21st-century Benedict Arnolds
  • Made in America
  • Cartoon by Matt Davies
  • Returning a right to states that they didn’t use in the first place
  • Cranky is in the eye of the beholder
  • Cool heads are needed now on Ukraine
  • A missing piece of information on the Montgomery County executive race
  • Ways to protect life in Virginia
  • Ask Eugene Robinson about the latest news around the U.S. and world
  • Chat with Perry Bacon Jr. about politics and beyond
  • Chat with Jennifer Rubin about her columns, politics, policy and more
  • Send a letter to the editor
  • Contact the reader representative
  • Submit an op-ed
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.content:

entry.title: Sydney Morning Herald: 5 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: Sydney Morning Herald: 5 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-05 03:53AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Sydney floods LIVE updates: Natural disaster funding announced for 23 LGAs as heavy rain continues across city; more evacuation orders issued
  • Masur reacts to Kyrgios 'tanking' tactic
  • Help, I’m addicted to Duolingo - und das ist nicht gut
  • Twelve arrested for alleged murder of eight-year-old child
  • Flood crisis worsens in Sydney
  • Police capture person of interest in Chicago parade shooting
  • ‘I became desperate’: the singer trying to recover her legacy from the ABC

entry.title: Guardian: 5 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • North Korea dam water release forces South Koreans near border to evacuate
  • Chicago mass shooting: man in custody after six shot dead at Fourth of July parade
  • New Zealand Cricket strikes pay equity deal so women and men earn same match fees
  • Russia-Ukraine war: what we know on day 132 of the invasion
  • Ukrainian flag arrives in Snake Island after Russian retreat
  • NSW floods: Sydney braces for two more days of flooding as state smashed by rain and wind
  • ’I’m terrified I might be here forever’: Brittney Griner appeals to Biden in letter
  • Edgbaston to investigate racist abuse of Indian supporters during England Test
  • UK cinemas ban fans in suits from Minions: The Rise of Gru
  • Premier League footballer arrested by Met police on suspicion of rape
  • Women’s Euro 2022: our writers predict the winners and surprises
  • Model pro Nick Kyrgios shocks again with polite and efficient victory | Barney Ronay
  • Parliament an unsafe workplace due to sexual misconduct by MPs, say unions

The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.
